description: Multi-tier author discovery and citation-based paper expansion strategy
rationale: Not all contributors are equal in terms of intellectual leadership vs. execution. We need a tiered system that (a) captures the full ecosystem of contributors, (b) identifies high-impact papers we've missed via citation graph analysis, and (c) avoids conflating a first-author PhD student with a PI who shaped the field.

tier_1_field_shapers.description: PIs/last authors who defined subfields or led landmark projects. Typically last/corresponding author on major papers. Their publication record IS the field's history.
criteria: Last/corresponding author on 3+ landmark connectomics papers
Led or co-led a major project (MICrONS, FlyEM, FlyWire, BRAIN CONNECTS)
Coined key terminology or invented foundational methods
examples: Seung
Lichtman
Sporns
Helmstaedter
Denk
Jain
Vogelstein
tier_1_field_shapers.search_strategy: Already captured in seed list. Expand via co-PI networks.

tier_2_key_contributors.description: First/co-first authors on landmark papers, senior staff scientists, or method developers. They did the core intellectual work on specific papers but may not run their own labs (yet).
criteria: First author on 1+ highly cited connectomics paper (>100 citations)
Developed a key tool/method used broadly (Neuroglancer, CAVE, FFN, etc.)
Staff scientist or senior engineer with sustained multi-year contributions
Often the person who actually built the thing
examples: Sven Dorkenwald - FlyWire proofreading, CAVE (now faculty-track)
Michal Januszewski - FFN first author at Google
Jeremy Maitin-Shepard - Neuroglancer creator at Google
Jan Funke - segmentation/synapse methods at Janelia
Srinivas Turaga - deep learning for EM at Janelia/HHMI
Amelio Vazquez-Reina - early automated segmentation
Verena Kaynig - segmentation pipelines at Harvard
Casey Schneider-Mizell - MICrONS proofreading lead at Allen
Shan Xu (C. Shan Xu) - FIB-SEM technology development at Janelia
Kenneth Hayworth - ATLUM, tape-collecting ultramicrotome
tier_2_key_contributors.search_strategy: Find via first-author position on existing corpus papers. Track forward citations.

tier_3_infrastructure_builders.description: Engineers, data scientists, and tool developers whose work enables the field but who may not be first/last author on the flagship papers.
criteria: Built or maintains critical infrastructure (bossDB, Neuroglancer, CAVE, webKnossos)
Middle author on many papers but essential for the work
examples: Brock Wester - bossDB lead, JHU APL infrastructure
Daniel Xenes - bossDB engineering
Dean Kleissas - bossDB, data pipelines
Jordan Matelsky - dotmotif, JHU APL tools
Eric Johnson - annotation standards, JHU APL
William Silversmith - cloud-volume, igneous, Princeton tools
Nico Kemnitz - segmentation infrastructure, Princeton
tier_3_infrastructure_builders.search_strategy: Mine author lists from infrastructure papers in existing corpus.

tier_4_emerging_voices.description: Early-career researchers with recent high-impact first-author papers who may become tier 1-2 in the next decade.
criteria: First author on recent (2022+) high-impact paper
Active in emerging subfield (foundation models, ExM connectomics, etc.)
examples: Benjamin Pedigo - graph statistics for connectomes (Vogelstein lab)
Michael Winding - larval Drosophila connectome first author
Jasper Phelps - GridTape TEM first author
Sahil Loomba - cortical interneuron connectomics (Helmstaedter lab)
Szi-chieh Yu - FlyWire annotation lead
tier_4_emerging_voices.search_strategy: Find via recent first-author positions on high-profile papers.

citation_discovery_pipeline.description: Use citation graph analysis to find important papers we're missing, starting from our existing 154-paper corpus.

step_1_forward_citations.description: For each of our 154 papers, find papers that cite them. Papers cited BY many of our corpus papers are likely important.
step_1_forward_citations.metric: Count how many of our 154 papers each citing paper references. Papers referencing 5+ of ours are strong candidates.
step_1_forward_citations.rationale: A paper that cites many papers in our corpus is likely in the same intellectual neighborhood.

step_2_cocitation_analysis.description: Find papers frequently co-cited with our corpus papers. If papers A and B from our corpus are often cited together with paper C, then C is likely relevant.

step_3_reverse_pagerank.description: Build citation graph among our corpus + their citations. Run PageRank to find papers that are central connectors in the citation network.
step_3_reverse_pagerank.method: Build adjacency matrix from citation data. Run PageRank. High-PR papers not in our corpus are discovery candidates.
step_3_reverse_pagerank.rationale: PageRank identifies papers that are not just cited a lot, but cited by OTHER highly-cited papers — the true connectors.
implementation_notes: Seed the graph with our 154 papers
Add 1-hop citations (papers citing ours + papers ours cite)
Run PageRank on the full graph
Rank papers NOT in our corpus by their PR score

step_4_author_bridging.description: Find authors who connect our seed experts. Co-authorship network analysis reveals bridging figures.
step_4_author_bridging.method: Build co-authorship graph from all papers. Find authors with high betweenness centrality who are not yet in our expert list.
step_4_author_bridging.rationale: Bridging authors connect different subcommunities (e.g., someone who co-authored with both Sporns AND Seung spans macro and nano connectomics).

step_5_dimension_gap_fill.description: For dimensions with low coverage (cell-types: 4, ethics-society: 2), specifically search for landmark papers.

underserved_dimensions.cell-types: Search for Patch-seq, single-cell transcriptomics x connectivity papers
underserved_dimensions.ethics-society: Search for data sharing policy, neuroethics of brain mapping, open science in connectomics
underserved_dimensions.neuroanatomy: Search for synapse classification, ultrastructure quantification papers

phase_1_api_harvest.description: Query Semantic Scholar for citation data on all 154 papers
phase_1_api_harvest.tool: Semantic Scholar API (when accessible) or OpenAlex API

phase_2_graph_analysis.description: Build citation graph, run PageRank, co-citation analysis
phase_2_graph_analysis.output: Ranked list of candidate papers not in corpus

phase_3_author_network.description: Build co-authorship graph, compute centrality measures
phase_3_author_network.output: Tiered author list with centrality scores

phase_4_enrichment.description: Add top candidates to corpus with tier labels
